items.title: N9 polls: Aminuddin prefers small-scale campaigns to better engage voters
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said he prefers a small-scale campaign approach to enable him to better understand the needs of the local community. Aminuddin, who is contesting the Linggi seat in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election under Pakatan Harapan (PH), said he has adopted this approach since 2008 to allow him to interact more effectively with voters, while making them feel more comfortable. "I feel for the voters.

items.title: Jalaluddin thanks Perikatan for backing BN to lead Negeri Sembilan if alliance wins
items.summary: JELEBU, July 19 — Negeri Sembilan Umno chief Datuk Jalaluddin Alias today thanked Perikatan Nasional (PN) for what he said was the coalition’s early decision to leave the state’s menteri besar post to Barisan Nasional (BN) should the two succeed in forming the next state government after the August 1 election. He said BN and PN had yet to discuss who should become menteri besar or how executive council positions would be allocated, with both coalitions choosing to focus on winning the election first. “We (BN and PN) have not discussed who the menteri besar candidate will be.

items.title: Yuneswaran calls for greater Indian representation in civil service
items.summary: jashadiqe@nst.com.my Caption: Deputy National Unity Minister R. Yuneswaran called for more Indian youths to pursue careers in the civil service. Courtesy pic: National Unity Ministry. SEGAMAT: More Indian youths should step forward and pursue careers in the civil service to increase the community's representation in the public sector, Deputy National Unity Minister R. Yuneswaran said.

items.title: Pocket-size AI: Powerful phones star at China show
items.summary: Wide adoption of phones running on so-called AI agents would be a revolution, but would also take control away from major apps, which aren't always happy about it. Smartphone maker Nubia unveiled its NaviX Ultra, a phone powered by Doubao, China's massively popular AI chatbot tool run by TikTok creator ByteDance. "A new era of AI agent smartphones begins," Nubia said, sharing images of the handsets online.

items.title: Pakistani man in Kelantan loses Proton Gen-2, fined RM20,000 after giving four undocumented migrants a ride
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 19 — A Proton Gen-2 ride cost a Pakistani man more than just a set of wheels after the Sessions Court in Pasir Mas, Kelantan today ordered the car forfeited to the government and fined him RM20,000 for allowing four undocumented migrants to remain inside it. Judge Zulkifli Abdullah imposed the fine on Muhammad Zaman, 30, and ordered him to serve eight months in prison if he fails to pay, Harian Metro reported. Muhammad pled guilty to allowing the four undocumented migrants to be in the car at a parking area along Jalan Parit Dalam in Kota Bharu at about 7.10am on July 12.

items.title: PJ's 223 food court traders plead for help after third flash flood this year
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Vendors at the 223 food court here are calling for government intervention after a flash flood yesterday. Jamaliah Yusoff, 62, said yesterday's disaster was the worst yet, with water rising to chin level, surpassing previous incidents on April 20 and May 6. The flood struck around 4pm yesterday, catching many by surprise.

items.title: PJ floods: Residents deserve answers and clear timeline, says MP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung has yet to receive a formal response from the local authority over the flash floods that hit parts of Petaling Jaya, Selangor, yesterday. "No formal response yet," Lee told the New Straits Times when asked if he had heard back from the council. Following that incident, Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ) said it was planning long-term upgrades , including floodwalls and a river overflow control system along Sungai Penchala and Sungai Klang.

items.title: Three teenage brothers, another teen held over motorcycle thefts in Sg Petani
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Police arrested four teenage boys, three of whom are brothers, on suspicion of stealing motorcycles around the Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd (KTMB) parking area near here on July 14. Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said preliminary investigations revealed that the suspects, aged 14 to 16, were known as the Vetri Gang and stole parked motorcycles in the area by pushing them as if they were broken. He said 20 cases of stolen motorcycles have been reported in the area.

items.title: Queen presents aid to police families, asnaf community
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, today officiated the Wilayah Prihatin Uniti Merdeka Contribution presentation ceremony at Dataran Merdeka. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's official Facebook page, Her Majesty was received on arrival by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's wife Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, the Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Marhamah Rosli, and Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus. Raja Zarith Sofiah presented contributions to 30 selected children of police officers and the asnaf community in the Federal Territory.

items.title: Cat rescued from ruins of Venezuela quake offers 'ray of hope'
items.summary: CARABALLEDA: A weakened cat was pulled from under the rubble of a collapsed residential complex in Venezuela on Saturday in what a rescuer described as a "ray of hope" in the aftermath of deadly earthquakes. Volunteer rescue worker Andres Carvajal said he spotted the cat during a search in a La Guaira housing complex that had been reduced to rubble. "We saw the cat, it got scared and went back inside, deeper into the building," Carvajal told AFP.

items.title: Loke advises Negri election machinery to prioritise road safety
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Road safety and traffic compliance must be strictly observed throughout the ongoing 16th Negri Sembilan election campaign, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said. The DAP secretary-general said he had personally instructed his party's campaign teams to wear helmets at all times when riding motorcycles during the campaign period. "We want to ensure this campaign stays accident-free.

items.title: Southern China braces for more rain, Chongqing landslide search continues
items.summary: BEIJING: China's meteorological authority on Sunday issued a heavy rain alert for parts of southern China, following a warning a day earlier of mountain flood risks in areas like Chongqing and Yunnan and potential emergency evacuations in vulnerable places. Cutting south China diagonally, the sprawling rain belt stretches from the southwestern province of Yunnan to the Yangtze River Delta in China's eastern coast. Rail authorities suspended some passenger trains on the Shanghai–Kunming railway on Sunday due to heavy rain, state broadcaster CCTV reported.

items.title: Pakistani man fined, car forfeited over undocumented migrants
items.summary: PASIR MAS: A Pakistani man was fined RM20,000 by the Sessions Court here today for allowing four undocumented migrants to remain in his car last Sunday (July 12). Muhammad Zaman, 30, pleaded guilty before Sessions Court Judge Zulkifli Abdullah. The four undocumented migrants were identified as Indian national Anil Kumar, 28, and three Pakistani nationals — Jaan Masih, 37, Ahmed Zahaib, 33, and Mohsin Ali, 23.

items.title: Immigration detains 17 foreigners in Pudu raid, uncovers suspected vice operation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department detained 17 illegal immigrants, including an Indonesian couple suspected of facilitating prostitution, during Op Gegar at the Fraser Business Park area in Pudu early yesterday morning. It said 17 foreigners, comprising 10 men and seven women from Indonesia, Bangladesh, and Myanmar, were arrested for suspected offences under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and the Immigration Regulations 1963. It involved 40 immigration officers, assisted by 10 officers from several enforcement agencies.

items.title: Defence Minister Koizumi says Japan needs debate on nuclear weapons
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi said that Japan needs a debate on nuclear weapons, as some European countries are seeking greater nuclear deterrence, Kyodo News reported. Koizumi cited France and Finland as pursuing policies that favour stronger nuclear deterrence. Koizumi said Japan's security environment has grown harsher and argued Japan needs to change a situation in which some topics are deemed unacceptable to debate.

items.title: Faith vs therapy: Inside the Philippine school for exorcists
items.summary: The Michael Center for Spiritual Liberation and Exorcism – five months old this week – was built because "cases were piling up", Father Jose Francisco Syquia told AFP. Trauma driven by bullying, sexual abuse and the pain of separation as family members leave for work abroad has increasingly opened the door to spiritual attacks, he said. "We had to find a more permanent and secure and private place to pray over people," Syquia said.

items.title: Zizie Izette to learn fate tomorrow in RM2.8 million corruption case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad will learn her fate tomorrow when the Sessions Court delivers its verdict on three charges of abetting in a RM2.8 million corruption case. Zizie Izette, 47, the wife of the late Kinabatangan member of parliament Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin, faces three charges of abetting her husband in obtaining cash bribes amounting to RM2.8 million linked to a RM150 million investment in Public Mutual Berhad unit trusts. On Nov 18, 2024, the Court of Appeal allowed the prosecution's appeal against the Sept 7, 2023 High Court decision acquitting the couple.

items.title: ‘No excuses’: Anwar to explain KWAP’s RM200m eFishery investment loss in Dewan Negara tomorrow
items.summary: IPOH, July 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he will explain in the Dewan Negara tomorrow the reported RM200 million loss involving Retirement Fund Incorporated’s (KWAP) investment in Indonesian company eFishery. According to Sinar Harian reported that Anwar, who is also finance minister, said he would set out the facts surrounding the investment and would not offer excuses over the matter. The ministry said the investor consortium, including KWAP, had initiated legal action and fund-recovery efforts, while reviewing internal governance and strengthening investment controls.

items.title: 2027 Budget may see higher Sara allocation, says PM
items.summary: IPOH: The government is considering increasing the allocation for the Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (Sara) initiative under Budget 2027, subject to the country's economic position, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said he had discussed the matter with Treasury secretary-general Datuk Johan Mahmood Merican to assess the feasibility of increasing the assistance to ease the people's burden. So when prices in India increase, our onion prices also go up," he said.

items.title: Johor Under-12 football tournament player dies during match
items.summary: The boy, identified as Danish Hizqeel Mohd Hanif, was a player with Shining Academy, a football club based in Pasir Gudang. Based on a Shining FC Facebook post, Danish "passed away while playing in a football tournament. Shining FC said Danish had represented the club since he was 8-years-old.

items.title: Private hospital doctor jailed seven years for physical sexual assault on boy
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A private hospital doctor was sentenced to seven years' jail and seven strokes of the rotan by the Sessions Court today after being found guilty of sexually assaulting an 11-year-old boy four years ago. Dr Mohd Syafwan Mohd Soffian, 42, was convicted on seven counts of physical sexual assault under the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017. For the remaining charge under Section 14(d), he was sentenced to seven years' jail, one stroke of the rotan and a two-year police supervision order.

items.title: Three teen girls held over viral 'Superman' motorcycle stunt
items.summary: KUANTAN: Three teenage girls who went viral for performing 'Superman' stunts on their motorcycles along Jalan Kuantan Bypass-Gebeng road last week have been arrested. Video clips showing the three girls lying flat on their motorcycles with both legs extended backwards while riding on a wet road surface circulated widely on social media, prompting police action. Kuantan police chief Assistant Commissioner Ashari Abu Samah said the girls, aged between 18 and 21, were arrested at 8pm yesterday after they turned up at the Kuantan Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department for questioning.

items.title: Onn Hafiz dismisses PH Youth resignation call over BN-PN understanding
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has dismissed calls by Pakatan Harapan Youth for Barisan Nasional ministers and deputy ministers to resign over the coalition's electoral understanding with Perikatan Nasional in the Negri Sembilan polls. Responding to the call, Onn Hafiz said PH should apply the same standard in states where BN and PH do not cooperate politically. Also present were Juasseh candidate Datuk Ismail Lasim, Seri Menanti candidate Sufian Maradzi, candidate Mohamad Qayyum Abd Jalil, Pilah candidate Lisa Mat Yassin and Johol candidate Datuk Saiful Yazan Sulaiman.

items.title: N9 polls: 'Treachery' claim against me doesn't make sense, says Jalaluddin
items.summary: JELEBU: Negri Sembilan Umno liaison committee chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias has questioned accusations by several Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders that he committed an act of treachery. He said the accusations were illogical. "Some PH leaders said I had committed treachery (derhaka).

items.title: Zizie Izette to learn verdict tomorrow in RM2.8m corruption abetment case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad will learn her fate tomorrow when the Sessions Court delivers its verdict on three charges of abetting in a corruption case involving RM2.8 million linked to a RM150 million investment in Public Mutual Berhad unit trusts. Zizie Izette, 47, the wife of the late Kinabatangan Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin, is facing three charges of abetting her husband in obtaining cash bribes amounting to RM2.8 million. Under the first charge, Zizie Izette is accused of abetting Bung Moktar, who was then the non-executive chairman of Felcra Berhad, in obtaining a RM2.2 million cash bribe from Public Mutual Berhad investment agent Madhi Abdul Hamid.

items.title: Anwar pledges RM15.3 million boost for Perak religious and pondok schools
items.summary: IPOH: The Madani government will continue to enhance and uplift religious and pondok schools nationwide, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He noted that several pondok schools and maahad, or religious secondary schools, had been equipped with new technologies to ensure students are prepared to acquire new knowledge. "We approved allocations for 115 pondok schools in Perak last year.

items.title: Malaysia sends 'very strong' diplomatic note over territorial claim
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Malaysia has sent a "very strong" diplomatic note to a country that claimed part of its territory belongs to it, Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said today. Without naming the country, Mohamad said the claim was baseless as the people of the territory had held a referendum recognised by the United Nations (UN), with the outcome subsequently affirmed through a United Nations Security Council resolution. Recently, I sent a very strong diplomatic note to the country concerned because it claimed that an area within our country belongs to them.

items.title: Woman fined RM5,000 for allowing undocumented migrant to stay at shop
items.summary: PASIR MAS: A woman was fined RM5,000 by the Sessions Court here today for allowing an undocumented migrant to remain at her sundry shop on July 9. The 30-year-old pleaded guilty to the charge before Sessions Court Judge Zulkifli Abdullah. According to the charge, Nor Hapiza Zakaria allowed a 23-year-old Pakistani man, identified as Asif Iqbal, to remain at Cikk Za Mini Mart, PT 9767, Kampung Pusu 40, Cherang Hangus, here at 12.30pm on July 9.

items.title: From AI prompting to vehicle maintenance, thousands of free courses offered to Malaysians during National Training Week
items.summary: SANDAKAN, July 19 — Thousands of free courses and skills development activities will be offered to all Malaysians, regardless of age, in conjunction with the fourth edition of the National Training Week (NTW) 2026, which begins tomorrow. The Human Resource Development Corporation (HRD Corp), in a statement today, said the programme, organised in collaboration with the Ministry of Human Resources (Kesuma), aims to strengthen lifelong learning, and information can be accessed at www.nationaltrainingweek.gov.my . “The NTW is not merey an annual programme, but serves as a national movement to instil a culture of lifelong learning among Malaysians,” Khairul Firdaus said when launching the programme.

items.title: PM to explain KWAP's eFishery investment in Dewan Negara tomorrow
items.summary: IPOH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will address questions surrounding Retirement Fund Incorporated (KWAP) and its investment in eFishery when he appears in the Dewan Negara tomorrow. Anwar said he would provide a detailed explanation based on facts rather than making premature comments on the matter. Let us present the facts tomorrow in the Dewan Negara," he said.

items.title: Jalan Tun Razak tree fall victim to be laid to rest in Batu Pahat
items.summary: His younger brother, Mohd Amir Adham Saion, 38, said the family made the decision following the completion of the post-mortem examination at Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL). He said the arrangement would allow their elderly parents, who remained in their hometown, to see their son for the final time. Amir Adham said his brother was a well-liked person who was always willing to help others, including family members and friends.

items.title: ‘Win, but not by breaking the law’: Loke urges road safety during Negeri Sembilan campaign
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — Road safety and traffic compliance must be strictly observed throughout the ongoing 16th Negeri Sembilan state election campaign, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke. The DAP secretary-general said he has personally instructed his party’s campaign teams to wear helmets at all times when riding motorcycles during the campaign period. “We want to ensure this campaign stays accident-free.

items.title: Putrajaya to Muar: Syed Saddiq says RM650,000 raised after completing 170km charity run
items.summary: MUAR, July 19 — Muar Member of Parliament (MP) Syed Saddiq Syed Abd Rahman today succesfully completed his 170-kilometre “Larian Kesyukuran” (Gratitude Run) at Laman Maharani here, marking the culmination of a charity mission that began on July 16. Syed Saddiq said the four-day charity run raised RM650,959.2, exceeding the initial target of RM200,000, and pledged that 100 per cent of the funds would be channelled towards welfare assistance for Muar constituents. He said the support extended beyond Muar residents, with people also coming from surrounding areas, including Batu Pahat.

items.title: 14 families keep Pulau Raya's 200-year heritage alive
items.summary: TANAH MERAH: Pulau Raya, one of Kelantan's smallest villages, is home to 14 Chinese families preserving a way of life that has endured for more than two centuries. Village head Wee Thin Kim, 51, said the island, believed to be more than 200 years old, had changed significantly over the decades. He said Pulau Raya was once home to both Malay and Chinese families, but today only the Chinese community remains.

items.title: Samsung cuts US jobs, offers relocations ahead of HQ move
items.summary: SEOUL: Samsung Electronics has cut jobs at its US display, phone and other consumer electronics operations — affecting workers mainly in New Jersey and Texas, according to documents and two people familiar with the matter. The South Korean tech giant said on Sunday in a statement to Reuters that 739 roles in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey, have been affected by plans by Samsung Electronics America (SEA) — which is focused on consumer electronics and does not include chips — to move its headquarters to Texas. Samsung's decision to shift SEA's headquarters is striking because SEA employees in New Jersey only moved to new offices with much fanfare less than a year ago.

items.title: Syed Saddiq to focus on marriage before deciding on Muda leadership return [WATCH]
items.summary: MUAR: Muar member of parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman has yet to consider reclaiming the leadership of Muda following his recent court acquittal. Instead, the former youth and sports minister said he would focus on the next chapter of his life, which is building a family. Syed Saddiq is engaged to actress and singer Bella Astillah .

items.title: Pesona Metro bags RM247.5mil hospital job
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pesona Metro Holdings Bhd has secured a RM247.5 million contract to build a private hospital in Setia Alam, Selangor. Pesona Metro said its wholly-owned Pesona Metro Sdn Bhd had accepted a Letter of Award from Setia Alam Medical Centre Sdn Bhd (SAMC) for the execution and completion of piling, basement, main building and external works for the project. The proposed development comprises three hospital blocks with supporting facilities and multi-storey car parks, located in Setia Alam, Shah Alam, Selangor.

items.title: Heavy rain warning issued for KL, Selangor and nine other states until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to hit several states in the Peninsula, Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan until 5pm today. Similar warnings were also issued for Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya, Melaka, Selangor (Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang), Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah, Rembau and Tampin) and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat and Muar). In Sarawak, the areas involved are Kuching, Samarahan, Mukah (Matu, Dalat, and Mukah), Kapit (Song), and Miri (Subis, Telang Usan, and Miri), while in Sabah, it affects the Interior (Kuala Penyu) and Tawau (Tawau and Semporna).

items.title: ‘Thanks for the help’: Loke says MCA’s retreat boosts DAP in three Negeri Sembilan seats
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — DAP Secretary-General Anthony Loke today described MCA’s decision not to contest three of its traditional seats in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election as a move that indirectly benefits DAP by improving its chances of retaining those constituencies. “I would like to thank MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong for being so ‘generous’ in helping DAP by not contesting. He also expressed confidence that DAP would retain all 11 state seats it is contesting, contributing to PH’s bid to form the Negeri Sembilan state government.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan polls: Cousins turn political rivals in three-way contest for Klawang
items.summary: JELEBU, July 19 — The campaign trail at Kuala Klawang weekend market here took an interesting turn today when two cousins contesting the Klawang state seat in the Negeri Sembilan State Election crossed paths while engaging with voters. Incumbent Datuk Bakri Sawir of Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Danni Rais of Perikatan Nasional (PN) were seen engaging with visitors and traders at the market as campaigning entered its second day today. The Klawang state seat, which has 13,355 registered voters, will see a three-cornered fight involving Bakri, Danni and Bersatu’s Muhammad Adib Musa.

items.title: ‘Do not forget’: Anwar urges Malaysian youth to remember struggle for independence
items.summary: IPOH, July 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today urged the current generation to appreciate the country’s history of struggle and understand the meaning of independence that has been achieved through the sacrifices of previous generations. Speaking at the launch of the 2026 National Month and Fly the Jalur Gemilang Campaign (MPBKKJG 2026) here, Anwar said the struggle that demanded the blood, sweat and tears of past independence fighters should teach Malaysians to appreciate the comforts they enjoy today. Also present were Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil, National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang and Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad.

items.title: Ministry: Nearly 173,000 companies register for subsidised diesel scheme nationwide, covering 465,000 vehicles
items.summary: He said 28,127 companies involving 68,669 vehicles were registered in Sabah, Sarawak and the Federal Territory of Labuan following the expansion of the targeted diesel subsidy mechanism to the three regions on July 1. Of the total, 18,770 companies with 51,679 vehicles were registered under the land transport sector for consumer goods, while 7,241 companies involving 13,557 vehicles were registered under the company-owned land transport sector covering jeeps and pickup trucks. Another 2,116 companies involving 3,433 vehicles were registered under the public land transport sector.

items.title: Azalina: Over US$1.37b in 1MDB-linked assets returned by US, more still tied up overseas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — More than US$1.37 billion in assets linked to the misappropriation of 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) funds have been returned by the United States to Malaysia to date, said Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. “In addition to the assets that have been returned, a substantial amount of funds and assets linked to 1MDB remain detained, frozen or subject to forfeiture proceedings in the United States and several other foreign jurisdictions,” she said in a written parliamentary reply. On the exact value of the funds and assets that remain detained, frozen or subject to forfeiture proceedings, Azalina said it could not be determined precisely at this time.

items.title: Activist remanded for two days in Seremban after returning from Thailand
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — A 33-year-old activist has reportedly been remanded for two days to assist investigations into alleged threats made on social media in March. Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad reportedly said the man was detained to facilitate investigations under Section 506 of the Penal Code and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998. Police opened the investigation after receiving a police report on March 10 alleging that the suspect had made threatening posts Facebook .

items.title: Electrician loses RM150,000 in WhatsApp investment scam
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 19 — An electrician lost RM150,000 after falling victim to a non-existent investment syndicate early this month. Kuala Terengganu police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said the 48-year-old victim received a WhatsApp message on July 3 from a woman promoting an investment scheme known as “Centralize International”. He said the suspect persuaded the victim to participate by promising substantial returns within a short period.

items.title: Authorities confirm no one harmed in Melaka shophouse collapse
items.summary: MELAKA, July 19 — The Melaka Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) has confirmed that no one was trapped in the collapse of a two-storey shophouse that was undergoing renovation in Taman Kota Laksamana yesterday, following a search operation and verification with the relevant agencies. Melaka JBPM director Mohd Zaidi Ma’at said the confirmation was based on inspections and search operations conducted by firefighters at the scene, as well as investigations carried out jointly with the police, the Melaka Historic City Council (MBMB) and the building owner. “The fire and rescue team conducted a search at the collapse site and found that no victims were involved in the building collapse.

items.title: Police approve 19 permits as Negeri Sembilan campaigns get under way
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — A total of 19 permit applications for ceramah (political talks) and political campaign activities received since yesterday for the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election have been approved. Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the applications were approved after meeting all the stipulated requirements. “Overall, the security and public order situation throughout the 16h Negeri Sembilan State Election remains good and under control.

items.title: Medicine from the sky: MCMC and Health Ministry to begin drone deliveries in Tawau
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), in collaboration with the Health Ministry, will launch a pilot project using drones to deliver medicines in Tawau, Sabah, around September or October, expanding the role of the National Information Dissemination Centre (NADI) in healthcare delivery. MCMC NADI Office head Dr Mohd Arif Adenan said the initiative, under the NADI Smart Services programme, is expected to benefit residents in about 12 villages, particularly those in rural and remote areas who face difficulties obtaining regular medicine supplies. Mohd Arif said MCMC would consider expanding the initiative to other areas with similar needs if the pilot project achieves its intended objectives.

items.title: Rescuers halt search at wrecked Melaka shophouse amid fears of further collapse
items.summary: MELAKA, July 19 — The Melaka Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) has suspended search and rescue (SAR) operations at the site of a collapsed two-storey shophouse in Taman Kota Laksamana here yesterday until the status of all workers at the premises is confirmed, while taking into account the risk posed by the unstable structure. Melaka Tengah Fire and Rescue Station chief Senior Fire Superintendent II K. Karuppannan said the department would not risk the safety of its personnel by entering the collapsed structure until it is confirmed whether anyone remains trapped. The collapsed two-storey shophouse at Taman Kota Laksamana in Melaka, July 18, 2026.

items.title: Najib to undergo procedure to remove plaque near blood vessel, says Rosmah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak is scheduled to undergo a medical procedure to remove plaque located near a blood vessel, his wife Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor said. In a Facebook post last night, Rosmah thanked those who had contacted them to ask about Najib’s condition and offered prayers and words of encouragement. Rosmah also expressed her gratitude for the support shown towards her husband.

items.title: Man dies after tree falls on two cars along Jalan Tun Razak in KL
items.summary: Dang Wangi police chief ACP Sazalee Adam said the victim suffered injuries to both arms and internal bleeding in the chest. “The victim died while receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL) at 4am today,” he said in a statement. Sazalee said all of them are receiving treatment at HKL, and physical examinations found no external injuries.

items.title: Activist wanted over alleged Facebook threats returns from Thailand and surrenders
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — An activist suspected of making criminal threats via Facebook surrendered at the Port Dickson district police headquarters (IPD) yesterday after it was believed that he had fled to Thailand since March. Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the 33-year-old local man turned himself in at about 12.45pm after being in the neighbouring country for more than four months. “Investigations found that the police received a report from a man at 6.25pm, on March 10, after allegedly being threatened on the social media site by the suspect.

items.title: Gerakan sacks Tang Jay Son for contesting in Rahang on Bersatu ticket
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — Gerakan has expelled Tang Jay Son from the party with immediate effect after he contested under the Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) ticket in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. Gerakan secretary-general Wong Chia Zhen said in a statement yesterday that Tang’s action constituted a serious breach of party discipline as it was contrary to the principle of loyalty to the party. “Gerakan wishes to stress that every member is responsible for complying with the party constitution, respecting organisational decisions, and upholding party discipline and integrity at all times.

items.title: Small firms and local contractors to receive 300-litre monthly diesel quota through fleet cards
items.summary: Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said this flexibility in assistance is granted specifically for diesel vehicles registered under the names of partnership companies or sole proprietorships nationwide for their business use. They use those vehicles for their small business needs,” he said. He was met by reporters after officiating the Madani Foster Village Project at Kampung Sekalong, Menumbok, here yesterday.

items.title: Give Pakatan a stronger mandate to keep Negeri Sembilan on course, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) Chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has appealed for the consideration and trust of the people of Negeri Sembilan to grant a stronger mandate to PH in the upcoming State Election (PRN). The Election Commission (EC) informed that a total of 103 candidates have been confirmed to contest in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election to vie for 36 State Legislative Assembly (DUN) seats, after the nomination process concluded yesterday. PH dominates the contest by fielding 36 candidates, including Aminuddin in the Linggi state seat, followed by Barisan Nasional (BN) with 25 candidates, Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) with 24 candidates, and Perikatan Nasional (PN) with 11 candidates.

items.title: From an idea to 800 sterilisations: How Catsoterra is helping to curb the stray cat population in Penang
items.summary: It started out as Cats of Penang in April last year and was formally established as Catsoterra Berhad in November. To date, it has facilitated the sterilisation of almost 800 cats across Penang and subsidised more than RM50,000 worth of TNR services (as of July 8). Explaining the Catsoterra system, he said volunteers who join the platform pay an annual RM60 membership fee.

items.title: How ordinary Penangites are taking on the stray cat population, one sterilisation at a time
items.summary: Motivated by her love for cats, Fouziah has also taken in 40 cats and rented a space to house them. The assistant manager spends about RM2,500 a month on feeding and taking care of the 40 cats and the stray cats. Catsoterra, which was set up in Penang only last year, is a volunteer-driven animal welfare organisation that helps facilitate the sterilisation of stray cats in Penang.

items.title: PM Anwar calls on the people to share views via his AI avatar (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has invited the people, especially the young, to share their views, concerns and aspirations directly through his avatar to ensure that the country’s development continues to be based on the voices and wishes of the people. “So, I really hope that you can share your views, concerns and aspirations directly through this avatar because I am convinced that the country will only improve if it is based on the voices and wishes of the people,” he said. The Prime Minister said Malaysia was built by the aspirations, qualities and strengths of its people of various races and cultures, and invited all the people to participate in the campaign to create a fresh discourse for the country and the people.

items.title: Fractured alliances, multi-cornered fights shape Negeri Sembilan state election after nominations close
items.summary: A total of 103 candidates will contest all 36 state seats, with the Election Commission (EC) confirming 11 straight fights, 21 three-cornered contests, two four-cornered contests and two five-cornered battles. Another highlight battle is in Rantau, where Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan (BN) will defend his seat agaisnt PKR’s Dr Azizul Hakim Mahdi (PH). Attention will definitely be on Linggi, where outgoing Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun (PH) is seeking a fresh mandate after moving from his long-held Sikamat seat, to face a strong BN opponent Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli, and Bersatu’s Datuk Zamri Md Said.

items.title: Flash floods swamp Petaling Jaya after heavy rain (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Several areas in Petaling Jaya were hit by flash floods, including the 223 Food Court after heavy rain occurred in the Klang Valley this afternoon. Selangor Fire and Rescue Operations Division assistant director Ashrul Riezal Asbar said a team from Petaling Jaya Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the flooded area at 223 Food Court about 20 minutes after they received a call at 4.35 pm to find that the water level was almost two metres high. — Bernama Banjir kilat sekitar Mid Valley, Petaling Jaya, Kelana Jaya dan beberapa lokasi sekitar KL & Selangor.
